narrative: Market stress has dropped sharply from 30 to 12 over the past session, driven by a broad-based retreat in risk premia across equities and credit, with ten of eleven equity sectors in positive territory and no emerging market benchmarks in the red. High-yield spreads at 268 basis points sit at just the 5th percentile of their five-year range, signalling that credit markets are pricing near-peak complacency rather than residual concern. The standout move is the coordinated strength across emerging market equities, with EEM up 4.8%, INDA up 4.3%, and EWJ up 4.0% on a five-day basis, a breadth of gains that suggests the rotation carries conviction beyond a single idiosyncratic catalyst and warrants attention from allocators underweight non-US risk. Energy is the sole notable detractor, with XLE down 3.5%, likely reflecting softer commodity sentiment rather than any systemic deterioration given the otherwise clean risk backdrop. A sustained move in VIX back above 20, or a widening in high-yield spreads beyond the 350 basis point level, would be the clearest signal that the current calm-regime assessment requires reassessment.
